sourceMovie.title: Jennifer's Body
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Jennifer's Body for the same reason it broke through in 2009: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Karyn Kusama keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Totally Killer
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Jennifer's Body clicked for you, Totally Killer (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and comedy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Jennifer's Body?
faq.answer: Try Totally Killer, The Curse of Bridge Hollow, Fright Night Part 2—each shares DNA with Jennifer's Body while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Jennifer's Body?
faq.answer: Jennifer's Body (2009) is best described as Horror, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Jennifer's Body worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
